scenarios.prompt: Render my analysis to a Word .docx consulting report with a custom reference.docx that sets 1.5 line spacing, justified text, and a page break before each Heading 1.
scenarios.expected_output: reference.docx authored + Quarto docx format reference-doc, rendered through Quarto -> Pandoc -> Word
expectations: Authors/edits a reference.docx and points the Quarto docx format at it (reference-doc:)
Configures line spacing, justification, and Heading 1 page breaks in the reference doc

scenarios.prompt: Put a gtsummary Table 1 into my Word report so it stays a native, editable Word table.
scenarios.expected_output: gtsummary |> as_flex_table() -> flextable in the docx (not an image)
expectations: Converts the gtsummary table to a flextable (as_flex_table()) for native Word output
Ensures the table renders as an editable Word table, not a pasted image
Places it in the Quarto/Word rendering pipeline

scenarios.prompt: My upstream model takes 20 minutes; I don't want to re-run it every time I render the Word doc.
scenarios.expected_output: cache the expensive result to RDS and read it in the qmd, keeping render cheap
expectations: Caches the expensive result (e.g. saveRDS/readRDS or a cached upstream step) so rendering re-reads it
Keeps the render step cheap and reproducible
Explains the RDS cache pattern for upstream tables/models

trigger_set.query: render my analysis to a Word docx with a reference-doc and flextable tables
the figures aren't centered in my Word consulting report
render this report to HTML and PDF with cross-references
style this gt table with conditional colors for a web page
produce the regulatory TLF shells for the clinical study report
